siderable, l am satisfied I can put
through the project at a cost of - per
acre as compared to $40 per acre for

OF course in some instances the cost
would be many times greater than this,
but the results would more than make

"At Los Angeles, in the '70s, orig
mated the scheme for subterranean
dam, and the success of that project
is known throughout the world. The
same conditions that pertained in Los
Angeles pertain to the streams flow
ing within the mountains of this state.
All that is needed Is a project to store
up the water. l have reached solu.
ton of the problem. 1t is my intention
to keep hammering away at this mat
ter until l get the attention of con
gress. There is no doubt in my mind
but that millions of acres of land in
the northwest that are now considered
too high to benefit from irrigation
reservoirs can be made useful in this
way. 1t is matter that has to be
taken up sooner or later, and the SOON
err the better il will be for this state. !"
